title: Customers With High Average Spending
description: Find customers whose average order amount exceeds 250 and who have placed at least three orders. Return the customer name along with their average order amount.
sql_solution: SELECT c.name, AVG(o.amount) avg_amount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.name HAVING AVG(o.amount) > 250 AND COUNT(*) >= 3;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *

joined_orders = (
    customers.alias("c")
    .join(orders.alias("o"), col("c.id") == col("o.customer_id"), "inner")
    .select(
        col("c.name").alias("name"),
        col("o.amount").alias("amount")
    )
)

result = (
    joined_orders.groupBy("name")
    .agg(
        avg("amount").alias("avg_amount"),
        count(lit(1)).alias("order_count")
    )
    .filter((col("avg_amount") > 250) & (col("order_count") >= 3))
    .select("name", "avg_amount")
)
